visual_systems.typography_contract: One cinematic phrase dominates the slide; support text is sparse, secondary, and routed away from the focal field.
visual_systems.composition_contract: Use full-bleed or near-full-bleed fields, asymmetric proof rails, and one quiet zone so the slide does not read as a dashboard.
visual_systems.asset_strategy_contract: Draw original native stage fields, rules, proof marks, and labels; no copied source layouts or source brand surfaces.
visual_systems.motion_sequence_contract: Reveal field, then proof object, then route labels; keep the final field as the pause state.
forbidden_sameness_patterns: same visual family as Run 2.9 light cards
visual_systems.public_demo_first_read_probe: Thumbnail should read as a keynote/editorial moment before labels are legible.
visual_systems.anti_copy_boundary: Derived rules only; do not copy source layout, source UI, screenshots, frames, transcripts, logos, or brand marks.

visual_systems.typography_contract: A compact command headline and no more than three annotation labels should frame the product surface.
visual_systems.composition_contract: Product proof is staged as a layered object with depth, crop, offset labels, and non-uniform annotation routes.
visual_systems.asset_strategy_contract: Use native editable product shells, screen-like surfaces, connectors, and evidence chips, never borrowed UI screenshots.
visual_systems.motion_sequence_contract: Use product, evidence attachment, and decision state as the visible sequence.
forbidden_sameness_patterns: same visual family as Run 2.9 product rectangles
visual_systems.public_demo_first_read_probe: The viewer should immediately see a product system being demonstrated, not a report section.
visual_systems.anti_copy_boundary: Derived rules only; do not copy source UI, screenshots, frames, transcripts, logos, or brand marks.

visual_systems.typography_contract: Headline line breaks are intentional design objects; short evidence words sit on routes, not inside body-copy blocks.
visual_systems.composition_contract: Whitespace must be visible and purposeful, with one side of the slide left quiet or used as a tension field.
visual_systems.asset_strategy_contract: Use native type, rules, line marks, and small proof glyphs; decorative filler fails the memory.
visual_systems.motion_sequence_contract: Route phrase fragments through problem, proof, and gate states.
forbidden_sameness_patterns: same visual family title plus card
visual_systems.public_demo_first_read_probe: The headline should feel designed and launch-grade at a glance.
visual_systems.anti_copy_boundary: Derived rules only; do not copy source layout, screenshots, transcripts, logos, or brand marks.

visual_systems.typography_contract: Use low-word sequence labels; the motion structure should carry meaning before text does.
visual_systems.composition_contract: Use diagonal, radial, or scale-based sequence paths instead of rows of same-size rectangles.
visual_systems.asset_strategy_contract: Use editable sequence objects, pause markers, and state changes; no copied frames or video extracts.
visual_systems.motion_sequence_contract: Anticipation, scale-up, lock-in, and release must be visible as native objects and traceable steps.
visual_systems.public_demo_first_read_probe: The slide should visibly build toward a reveal even before animation support exists.
visual_systems.anti_copy_boundary: Derived rules only; do not copy source frames, source video, screenshots, transcripts, logos, or brand marks.

visual_systems.typography_contract: Labels stay short and attached to proof-path nodes; explanatory copy is routed out of the main slide.
visual_systems.composition_contract: Use arcs, diagonals, wedges, and negative-space corridors to make proof movement visible without equal panels.
visual_systems.asset_strategy_contract: Use editable native path geometry, connectors, wedges, labels, and proof groups.
visual_systems.motion_sequence_contract: Path nodes become future reveal steps and converge into the climax or decision object.
visual_systems.public_demo_first_read_probe: The proof should read as a path, not a table, chart, or report grid.
visual_systems.anti_copy_boundary: Derived rules only; do not copy source diagrams, screenshots, frames, transcripts, logos, or brand marks.
